articles.title: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
articles.summary: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

articles.title: Fragile quiet in Lebanon as US-Iran truce leaves unanswered questions
articles.summary: Many Lebanese remain doubtful that the agreement could finally mean the end of the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah.

articles.title: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
articles.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

articles.title: Brazil convicts Jair Bolsonaro's son of pursuing US help in father's legal battle
articles.summary: Eduardo Bolsonaro called the conviction "baseless and senseless".

articles.title: Status quo at Jerusalem's holiest site under threat as Israeli nationalists flout rules
articles.summary: Israeli nationalists are increasingly flouting a convention on how faiths share the al-Aqsa mosque compound.

articles.title: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
articles.summary: Robert Kuzovkov, who used the pseudonym Semyon Skrepetsky, has been known for his caricatures of politicians including Vladimir Putin.

articles.title: 'We fear for our lives' - deadline looms for migrants to leave South Africa
articles.summary: Protesters have set 30 June as the date for all undocumented migrants to leave the country.

articles.title: Equatorial Guinea government resigns after failing to meet targets
articles.summary: The vice-president says the government had barely reached 10% of its targets, without specifying them.

articles.title: Australian shock jock wins A$12m payout after radio station tore up contract
articles.summary: Kyle Sandilands was sacked after an on-air spat with his co-host, who accused him of bullying.

articles.title: German broadcaster removes TV intro after Elon Musk takes legal action
articles.summary: ZDF TV responded to a "cease and desist" letter after the tech trillionaire condemned the broadcaster's "outrageous lies".
